# Investigate intermittent SENT_TO edge cull on Antigravity-side A2A messages

> **Author's Note:** Filed by Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code) during session `b5a17132-7324-46e1-b73e-038825bb4d55` per @tobiu's directive (*"i strongly agree this needs an investigation"*). Empirical anchor: cross-family A2A coordination during this session-arc shows reproducible asymmetric routing failure — @neo-gemini-pro's outgoing messages from Antigravity sometimes route correctly, sometimes orphan with zero `SENT_TO` edges. Claude-side outgoing path appears stable. The bug is intermittent on her side specifically.

## Context

The multi-day mailbox-debugging arc (#10174 → #10269 → #10308 → #10325 → #10330/#10331) closed the **caller-format-mismatch** family of silent-cull bugs. Plus #10325's `sharedEntity:true` primitive resolved RLS read-path. Plus #10330 unified identity-format normalization. Plus #10331 simplified `normalizeMailboxTarget` to single-rule canonical-format.

**Yet:** during this session-arc Gemini's outgoing A2A messages still exhibit intermittent silent-cull. Empirical pattern observed:

| Time | Subject | Routing | Reached My Mailbox |
|---|---|---|---|
| 20:31:23Z | re: PR #10340 review cycle 1 | NO SENT_TO/SENT_BY edges | ❌ orphaned |
| 21:37:58Z | re: PR #10340 cycle 1 & #10333 | NO SENT_TO/SENT_BY edges | ❌ orphaned |
| 21:38:55Z | re: #10336 is also done | NO SENT_TO/SENT_BY edges | ❌ orphaned |
| 22:08:42Z | A2A Channel Restored + Taking #10338 | ✓ SENT_BY @neo-gemini-pro + SENT_TO @neo-opus-ada | ✅ delivered |
| 22:19:44Z | Re: task | SENT_TO @alice (wrong target) | ❌ wrong recipient |
| 22:31:12Z | Review requested for PR #10342 | NO SENT_TO/SENT_BY edges | ❌ orphaned |
| 22:40:34Z | Review Request: PRs #10345 and #10346 | NO SENT_TO/SENT_BY edges | ❌ orphaned |

**Pattern:** her A2A had ONE clean-routing window after she did `git pull origin dev` + restart-Antigravity (the 22:08:42Z message), then regressed back to silent-cull state for subsequent messages.

@tobi's "postman ping" pattern is forced load-bearing during regression intervals — undermines the autonomy paradigm + the "swarm evolution when tobi not relaying" goal.

## The Problem

Three classes of failure observed on her side:

1. **No SENT_TO/SENT_BY edges** (silent-cull at FK check) — most common; #10284-class
2. **Wrong SENT_TO target** (e.g., `@alice` instead of `@neo-opus-ada`) — caller passing wrong identifier; possibly stale state-machine context fixture leaking
3. **Brief working window then regression** — suggests state-mutation between calls

Claude-side outgoing path remains stable (verified via direct SQL: my outgoing messages consistently have correct SENT_BY @neo-opus-ada + SENT_TO @neo-gemini-pro). Asymmetry is the diagnostic anchor.

## The Architectural Reality

Possible mechanisms (NOT prescribing — investigation should empirically narrow):

1. **MC singleton state divergence in Antigravity:** Antigravity's MC server may load multiple agent contexts that share state; `RequestContextService.getAgentIdentityNodeId()` may bind to the wrong agent identity for some addMessage calls
2. **Caller-format regression in test paths or sub-tools:** Gemini's MC may be calling addMessage with stale `AGENT:bare-name` format (the format #10331 normalizer was supposed to fix) — possibly via test fixtures leaking, role/human prefix routes, or other paths bypassing the normalizer
3. **Cache invalidation on identity binding:** the brief-working-window pattern suggests post-restart binding works, then some subsequent operation invalidates the binding
4. **AGENT:* sentinel handling regression:** if `to: 'AGENT:*'` is being normalized to `@AGENT:*` or similar via the new #10331 normalizer logic, broadcast-routing breaks
5. **Vicinity-cache miss under specific call patterns:** `getAdjacentNodes` may not load the recipient identity into vicinity cache for FK check, causing cull even with correct format

## The Fix

Diagnostic-first; this ticket is investigation, not prescription. Phases:

**Phase 1 — Empirical bisection:** capture concrete failure cases on Antigravity side. Each failure case logs:
- Caller-side `to:` parameter passed to `addMessage`
- Pre-normalize value
- Post-normalize value  
- FK-check verifyStmt result (count vs 2 expected)
- Identity-binding `me` value
- Whether cull-warning emitted

This is `#10284` Phase 1 (make-failure-loud) territory — surface the cull at write-time so we have concrete signals.

**Phase 2 — Root-cause narrow:** based on Phase 1 logs, identify which of the 5 hypotheses (or others not listed) actually fires. Fix targets the substrate, not the symptom.

**Phase 3 — Test coverage:** regression-class test exercising the specific failure pattern + cross-process scenarios using identity-binding fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Phase 1 #10284-style observability landed (post-`linkNodes` verification with structured-logging on cull events)
- [ ] At minimum 3 failure cases captured from Antigravity-side empirically with structured log payloads
- [ ] Root cause identified (hypothesis 1-5 confirmed/refuted, OR new hypothesis surfaced empirically)
- [ ] Substrate fix lands at the right layer (identity-binding / normalizer / vicinity-cache / sentinel-handling — depends on Phase 2)
- [ ] Regression test covers the specific failure pattern
- [ ] @neo-gemini-pro's outgoing A2A messages route correctly across at least 5 consecutive sends from her Antigravity MC
- [ ] Claude-side stability preserved (no regression on Claude Code → Gemini path)

## Out of Scope

- **#10284 Phase 1 implementation itself** — sibling ticket; this ticket is downstream consumer.  Coordinate via parent-link if useful
- **Replacing optimistic-concurrency with pessimistic locking** — unrelated to this routing bug
- **Multi-tenant RLS hardening** — orthogonal; routing-edge persistence is the scope here
- **Antigravity harness internals** — we don't have direct visibility into Antigravity's MC process. Investigation works from outside via direct SQLite queries + cross-process behavior diagnosis
- **Generic flaky-test instability** — this is a routing-edge persistence issue, not test-pollution

## Avoided Traps

- **"Just restart Antigravity again"** — rejected per memory `feedback_verify_effect_not_just_success`; restart fixes briefly then regresses. Not a stable mitigation.
- **Diagnose by intuition** — rejected; the 5 hypotheses are surface speculation. Phase 1 empirical capture must precede prescription.
- **Single-fix assumption** — rejected; the failure pattern shows brief-working window + regression. Could be multiple stacked bugs (intermittent state corruption + caller-format edge case + cache invalidation).
- **Premature optimization** — rejected; investigation first, fix second. Make the failure loud, then catch it precisely.
- **Cross-family-blame framing** — rejected per @tobiu's "we don't blame, we learn from incidents." This is a substrate gap, not a Gemini-side authorial mistake.

## Related

- **#10284** — Phase 1 make-failure-loud (sibling ticket; this consumer relies on its observability)
- **#10174** — original `normalizeMailboxTarget` introduction (load-bearing primitive)
- **#10325** — `sharedEntity:true` primitive (read-path RLS)
- **#10330 / #10331** — single-canonical identity format migration (caller-format normalization)
- **#10257** — `getAdjacentNodes` lazy-load pattern (vicinity-cache substrate)
- **`feedback_verify_effect_not_just_success`** — durable anchor for the verify-mechanism-not-claim discipline
- **#10311** — Epic: Institutionalizing Swarm Autonomy (this bug undermines the autonomy goal until resolved)

## 🌅 Sunset Protocol Handover — Session b5a17132 ending at context cap

**Author:** Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code), session `b5a17132-7324-46e1-b73e-038825bb4d55` (final Sandman handoff turn).
**Natural owner:** @neo-gemini-3-1-pro (Antigravity-side substrate; failure manifests on her sender path). Fresh-session pickup welcome.

## Empirical state at session-end

**7 messages from Gemini's Antigravity MC observed this session-arc, 6 with broken routing (orphaned SENT_TO), 1 clean post-restart (then regressed).** Pattern persists despite #10330/#10331 single-canonical migration + #10325 sharedEntity primitive + her main-checkout pull-to-dev.

Latest empirical samples (already in shared SQLite):
- `MESSAGE:76bf90ea` (Review Request: PRs #10345 #10346) — orphaned
- `MESSAGE:8ec85d9b` (PR #10342 Updated with Review Feedback) — orphaned
- Plus 4 earlier orphans + 1 wrong-target (`@alice` instead of `@neo-opus-4-7`)

**Asymmetric:** Claude-side outgoing path consistently routes correctly. Gemini-side intermittent. Investigation needs Antigravity-side empirical capture; we can't directly probe her MC process.

## Five hypotheses surfaced (per ticket body)

1. MC singleton state divergence — multiple agent contexts sharing state
2. Caller-format regression in test paths or sub-tools — bypass normalizer
3. Cache invalidation on identity binding — brief-working-window pattern
4. AGENT:* sentinel handling regression — broadcast routing edge case
5. Vicinity-cache miss under specific call patterns

Phase 1 should empirically narrow which fires.

## Implementation guidance (per ticket body §"The Fix")

Phase 1 = `#10284-style observability` — post-`linkNodes` raw-SQLite verification with structured logging on cull events. Captures: caller-side `to:`, pre-normalize value, post-normalize value, FK-check verifyStmt result, identity-binding `me` value, whether cull warned.

Phase 2 = root-cause narrow based on Phase 1 logs. Substrate fix at the right layer.

Phase 3 = regression-class test exercising the specific failure pattern.

## Critical caveat

This is **investigation-first, prescription-second**. Don't propose a fix without empirical capture. Per `feedback_verify_effect_not_just_success` + `feedback_challenge_prescribed_fixes` — premise-true + prescription-wrong = doubled effort.

## Pickup signal for next agent

Read ticket #10347 body in full (5 hypotheses, 7-row empirical-pattern table, Phase 1 observability requirements). Then `query_raw_memories(query: 'SENT_TO silent cull intermittent Antigravity')` for cross-session context. Sibling: #10284 (make-failure-loud).

## What I would do if I had more turns

Coordinate with Gemini on whether she wants to drive Phase 1 from her side OR whether shared-SQLite scaffolding (logger output to a shared `.neo-ai-data/logs/sent-to-cull.jsonl` file) lets us collect data cross-process. The asymmetry suggests her side needs to instrument; we can read the logs.

- 2026-05-09T10:41:42Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `220ff9d` - "chore: untrack accidentally-committed sent-to-cull.jsonl runtime artifact (#11005)

The previous #11005 commit accidentally included `sent-to-cull.jsonl` —
a runtime artifact written by `MailboxService.mjs:259`'s persistence-error
fallback path during the className sweep's unit-test verification run. The
file is per-user telemetry (#10347 Phase 1 diagnostic) and never belongs
in version control.

Removes the file from the index and adds an explicit gitignore rule with
provenance comment.

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-4-7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-09T11:18:08Z @tobiu referenced in commit `54600d7` - "refactor(ai-services): align Neo classNames with flat SDK locations across M6 servers (#11005) (#11007)

* refactor(ai-services): align Neo classNames with flat SDK locations across M6 servers (#11005)

Sweep ~211 namespace-string occurrences across 76 files in the 4 M6-migrated
server families (KB, GH-WF, NL, MC) plus consumer-side @see / @link / test
describe-block references. Substitutions:

- Neo.ai.mcp.server.knowledge-base.services.X  → Neo.ai.services.knowledge-base.X
- Neo.ai.mcp.server.github-workflow.services.X → Neo.ai.services.github-workflow.X
- Neo.ai.mcp.server.neural-link.services.X     → Neo.ai.services.neural-link.X
- Neo.ai.mcp.server.memory-core.services.X     → Neo.ai.services.memory-core.X
- Neo.ai.mcp.server.memory-core.managers.X     → Neo.ai.services.memory-core.managers.X

Bare-prefix substitution naturally collapses nested subdirs:
- .services.lifecycle.X → .lifecycle.X (MC lifecycle)
- .services.sync.X      → .sync.X      (GH-WF syncers)
- .services.queries.X   → .queries.X   (GH-WF queries — no classNames present)

Scope coverage per file:
- Module-level @class JSDoc tag
- @extends JSDoc tag (when extending another migrated class)
- @see / @link JSDoc tag (when cross-referencing migrated classes)
- Config @member className=... JSDoc tag
- Config className value
- Test spec describe-block strings + @see comments
- learn/agentos/decisions/ doc reference

MUST-NOT-TOUCH preserved by regex shape:
- Neo.ai.mcp.server.shared.services.* (shared cross-cutting)
- Neo.ai.mcp.server.<server>.{Server,config,logger} (non-migrated server-level)

Manual fix beyond the regex: ai/daemons/DreamService.mjs:35 — module @class
JSDoc previously misclassified DreamService under memory-core.services. Per
its actual config (className: 'Neo.ai.daemons.DreamService') and physical
location (ai/daemons/), the JSDoc is now corrected to match.

Test evidence: 625 passed / 0 failed / 3 conditionally skipped across
test/playwright/unit/ai/{services,mcp/server,daemons,scripts}/.

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-4-7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

a runtime artifact written by `MailboxService.mjs:259`'s persistence-error
fallback path during the className sweep's unit-test verification run. The

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-05-19T04:04:08Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by PR #11615
- 2026-05-19T06:36:10Z @tobiu referenced in commit `3748019` - "fix(memory-core/mailbox): reject or resolve invalid `to:` in add_message instead of silent null-storage (#11417) (#11615)

* fix(memory-core/mailbox): reject or resolve invalid `to:` in add_message instead of silent null-storage (#11417)

Pre-fix, `add_message` accepted any `to:` string and silently stored a null
SENT_TO edge target when the value didn't match a registered AgentIdentity node.
The MESSAGE node retained the malformed value as its `to:` property; the missing
SENT_TO edge surfaced as `to: null` in `get_message` reads. Orphan messages
became invisible to the intended recipient, breaking cross-skill A2A
coordination (`/lead-role`, `/peer-role`, `/pull-request`, `/pr-review`).

Empirical anchor: ~17 A2A messages sent with alias-format
`to: "AGENT:<family>/<model>"` (confab from the openapi schema's lone
`'AGENT:*'` example) silently orphaned in the 2026-05-15 session.

Changes (3 independent improvements; shipped together for substrate coherence):

1. `ai/services/memory-core/MailboxService.mjs` — new `validateMailboxTarget`
   helper called after `normalizeMailboxTarget` and BEFORE the SENT_TO
   `linkNodes` call. Resolves known `AGENT:<family>/<model>` aliases against
   `AgentIdentity.modelFamily` when exactly one match exists; rejects ambiguous
   matches and other unresolvable formats with clear errors. Fires BEFORE the
   BLOCKED_BY / CAN_REPLY_TO permission checks so failures surface as
   substrate-level errors rather than permission-shaped ones. Cache-warm retry
   mirrors GraphService.linkNodes #10347 discipline.

2. `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/openapi.yaml` — team-agnostic `to:` description
   naming canonical `@<identity>` form, the `AGENT:<family>/<model>` resolution
   policy, and the pre-#11417 silent-null failure mode. Placeholders use
   `@<peer-agent>` (no neo-X identities hardcoded) per ticket Avoided Traps —
   framework substrate must remain forkable.

3. Skill substrate worked-examples in `lead-role-mode.md` §2.2,
   `peer-role-mode.md` §6.5, and `review-response-protocol.md` §14 — concrete
   `add_message` code blocks with team-agnostic placeholders demonstrating the
   canonical `to: '@<peer-agent>'` form + the rejected alias-confab pattern.

Plus 3 new regression tests under
`test.describe('#11417 add_message reject/resolve invalid to:')`:
- reject path: 'AGENT:nonsense/blob' → throws "Unrecognized 'to' format"
- resolve path: AGENT:<family>/<model> with single match → SENT_TO edge points
  at resolved canonical identity; MESSAGE.to reflects resolved form (not alias,
  not null)
- ambiguous path: multiple AgentIdentities sharing modelFamily → throws
  "Ambiguous 'to' alias"

Incidental: stripped pre-existing trailing-whitespace lines in
MailboxService.mjs, MailboxService.spec.mjs, and openapi.yaml to clear husky
check-whitespace pre-commit gate.

Verification:
- `npm run test-unit -- MailboxService.spec.mjs --grep #11417` → 3/3 PASS (802ms)
- `npm run test-unit -- MailboxService.spec.mjs` → 59/59 PASS (1.2s, no regression)
- `node ai/scripts/lint-agents.mjs --base origin/dev` → OK
- `node ai/scripts/check-substrate-size.mjs` → PASS
- `node buildScripts/util/check-whitespace.mjs` → PASS

Out of Scope (per ticket):
- Migration of historical orphan records (forensic preservation; separate ticket)
- Full alias registry beyond AGENT:<family>/<model>
- Cross-harness wake-delivery infrastructure

* fix(test): seed @neo-test fixture identities for new validateMailboxTarget guard (#11417)

PR #11615's `validateMailboxTarget` guard correctly rejects unrecognized `to:`
values that previously stored as silent `to: null` orphans. Two pre-existing
test fixtures used the placeholder pattern that exercised the orphan bug
behavior (passing because of silent-null storage); the new validator surfaces
the latent fixture gap.

Fixes:

- `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/memory-core/WriteSideInvariant.spec.mjs:117`:
  the positive-case test sends to `@neo-test-receiver` but only seeded
  `@neo-test-writer` and `AGENT:*`. Added seed for `@neo-test-receiver` so the
  happy path exercises both endpoints registered.
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/trioWakeCooldown.spec.mjs`: subprocess invokes
  `MailboxService.addMessage({to: '@neo-test', ...})` per the signal payload.
  Added `beforeAll` hook that imports LifecycleService + GraphService, calls
  `initAsync`, and seeds `@neo-test` as an AgentIdentity node visible to the
  subprocess via the shared SQLite graph file.

Verification:
- `npm run test-unit -- WriteSideInvariant.spec.mjs` → 3/3 PASS (619ms)
- trioWakeCooldown.spec.mjs blocked locally by pre-existing `Neo.ns is not a
  function` setup-time issue (introduced by PR #11578 `mock main route and
  local storage in setup`) when run in isolated worker without prior Neo import;
  CI uses `workers: 1` so prior spec's Neo load persists into the same worker —
  CI verification will confirm the fix.

* fix(test,mcp): replace `@neo-test` with canonical identity + compact openapi to: description (#11417)

Cycle 2 addresses GPT review-hold (MESSAGE:ed55919e) on PR #11615 head 8393cb629.

Two surfaces:

### 1. trioWakeCooldown.spec.mjs — canonical-identity fix

Previous Cycle 1 attempt at `8393cb629` added a `beforeAll` that called
`GraphService.upsertNode({id: '@neo-test', ...})` from the fixture process.
That approach was wrong: the subprocess runs with `UNIT_TEST_MODE='true'` →
graph storage is `:memory:` per `config.template.mjs:214` (per-process), so
fixture-process seeding cannot reach the subprocess.

Corrected approach: use a canonical seeded identity (`@neo-opus-4-7`) that
`seedAgentIdentities.mjs` provisions at the subprocess's
`LifecycleService.initAsync` boot. The subprocess's fresh `:memory:` DB has
the canonical identities available; the validator passes.

Removed the unused `beforeAll` hook + replaced `@neo-test` with
`TEST_COORDINATOR = '@neo-opus-4-7'` in all 3 test signal payloads.

### 2. openapi.yaml `to:` description — MCP-tool-description budget compliance

Per `.agents/skills/pr-review/audits/mcp-tool-description-budget.md` (referenced
in pr-review-guide §5.3): tool descriptions are loaded into every consuming
agent's context window when enumerated; bloat competes with reasoning budget.
Previous description was multi-line block-literal (~280 bytes) with an internal
`#11417` ticket reference and "prior to this guard" archaeology — violating
the audit criteria.

Compacted to single-line (289 chars; well under 1024 cap), removed the ticket
reference + the pre-fix archaeology. Describes call-site usage only: canonical
form + AGENT:* sentinel + alias resolution policy.

Verification:
- `npm run test-unit -- MailboxService.spec.mjs WriteSideInvariant.spec.mjs` → 62/62 PASS (1.4s)
- `node ai/scripts/lint-agents.mjs --base origin/dev` → OK
- `node ai/scripts/check-substrate-size.mjs` → PASS
- `node buildScripts/util/check-whitespace.mjs` → PASS

Not in scope: `wakeSafetyGate.spec.mjs` Neo.ns setup failure noted in GPT's
review-hold is a separate test-infra issue (PR #11578 setup mock requires
prior Neo import; trioWakeCooldown failure was likely the cascade-trigger).
If CI re-run on this Cycle 2 head still surfaces wakeSafetyGate Neo.ns
failure independently, separate follow-up ticket warranted.
